# HSBC — Usage Guide

*Mode: agent · 🤖 needs an LLM endpoint · requires: tesseract, poppler*

Consolidates a folder of HSBC statement PDFs into one clean, reconciled Excel workbook: it OCRs scanned pages (Tesseract + Poppler) while skipping pages that already have text, extracts the transaction ID, splits out the transaction date, scrubs the description, and adds an Extra Information column plus a Summary sheet.

## When to use it

Use this when you have one or more HSBC statement PDFs (savings or Premier) and want a single tidy, reconciled spreadsheet. For account-mapped output into GnuCash, use GnuCash Import and choose HSBC instead.

## Inputs

- **Folder containing HSBC statement PDF(s)** (required) — accepts: A folder of HSBC PDF statements.
  - A folder containing one or more HSBC statement PDFs.
  - ⚠️ Select the folder, not a single PDF. Scanned PDFs are OCR'd, so the run takes longer and needs Tesseract + Poppler (bundled in the frozen build).

## How to run

1. Put your HSBC PDFs in one folder.
2. Select that folder.
3. Click Run — OCR may take a minute on scanned statements.
4. Download the Excel workbook.

## Output

Output is written to the **`Data/outputs/`** folder, named:

```
Data/outputs/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-HSBC.xlsx
```

Files produced:

- **`YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-<input>-HSBC.xlsx`** — A reconciled workbook: one row per transaction with extracted transaction ID, separate transaction date, scrubbed description and an Extra Information column, plus a Summary sheet that ties the totals out.

## Tips

Check the Summary sheet first — if its totals reconcile, the extraction is sound. This skill is on the roadmap to go fully offline (no LLM).

## Troubleshooting

| If… | Then… |
|------|-------|
| Run errors mentioning the endpoint / model. | Currently HSBC uses an LLM step. Start Ollama or set an endpoint in Settings. |
| OCR output is poor or missing. | Ensure Tesseract and Poppler are available (PATH in source mode); use better-quality scans. |
